title: 0/1 Knapsack
description: Given n items with weights and profits, and a knapsack with capacity W, find the maximum profit achievable without exceeding capacity. Each item can be used at most once.
key_concept: dp[i][w] = max profit using first i items with capacity w. Either skip item i or take it (if weight fits). Bottom-up 2D DP or 1D optimized.
when_best: Items used at most once, capacity constraint, partition problems, subset sum variants

hints.L1: Define dp[i][w] = max value using items 0..i-1 with capacity w. For each item, try including vs excluding.
hints.L2: dp[i][w] = max(dp[i-1][w], profit[i-1] + dp[i-1][w-weight[i-1]]) if weight[i-1] <= w, else dp[i-1][w]. Can optimize to 1D array iterating w from W down to 0.
hints.L3: 1D optimization: dp=[0]*(W+1); for each item: for w from W down to item.weight: dp[w]=max(dp[w], item.profit+dp[w-item.weight]); return dp[W]
